en: And I can promise you that the Silicon Valley sluts don't care what anyone's relationship status is on Facebook.
Please, open your present. Your phone does work.
It's Mark. Okay, this is going to be tricky. Open your present.
ko: 좋아, 이건 좀 복잡할 거야. 어, 선물을 열어봐.

en: It's a silk scarf. Have you ever seen me wear a scarf?
This will be your first. Yeah. You froze our account?
I did. You froze the account? I had to get your attention, Mark.
ko: 네가 계정을 동결했어? 네 주목을 끌어야 했어, 마크.

en: Do you realize that you jeopardized the entire company?
ko: 네가 회사 전체를 위험에 빠뜨렸다는 걸 알고 있어? 네 행동이 내가 그동안 해온 모든 것을 영구적으로 파괴할 수 있었다는 걸 알고 있어?

en: Do you realize that your actions could have permanently destroyed everything I've been working on?
We have been working on it. Without money, the site can't function.
Okay, let me tell you the difference between Facebook and everybody else. We don't crash, ever.
ko: 좋아, 페이스북과 다른 모든 것의 차이를 말해줄게. 우리는 절대 다운되지 않아.

en: If the servers are down for even a day, our entire reputation is irreversibly destroyed.
Look, I under... Users are fickle. Friendster has proved that.
Even a few people leaving would reverberate through the entire user base.
ko: 전체 사용자 기반에 영향을 미칠 거야. 사용자들은 서로 연결되어 있어.

en: The users are interconnected. That is the whole point.
College kids are online because their friends are online. And if one domino goes, the other dominoes go.
ko: 대학생들이 온라인에 있는 이유는 친구들이 온라인에 있기 때문이고, 만약 하나의 도미노가 넘어지면,

en: Don't you get that? I am not going back to the Caribbean night at AEPI.
ko: 나는 85년 카리브해의 밤으로 돌아가지 않을 거야.
